key_insight: D-gun Al2O3-TiO2 coating substantially improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest predicts wear most accurately from sliding conditions.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical route to extend the service life of aerospace, biomedical, and marine titanium components using a hard ceramic surface layer plus data-driven wear prediction.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V by detonation gun spraying.
Performed pin-on-disc wear tests per ASTM G99 at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Trained ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from test parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM and wear tests showed reduced wear and tribo-layer formation on coated samples, while random forest had the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The ML models are only as reliable as the experimental range used here, so predictions may not generalize well outside the tested load, speed, and duration window.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
